name: Jiva Gosvami

dates_evidence.description: Manuscript colophons across the Ṣaṭ-Sandarbha and Sarva-Saṃvādinī.

core_thesis:
Jīva Gosvāmī is the Gauḍīya school's systematic theologian. The *Ṣaṭ-Sandarbha* raises Caitanya's experiential teaching and the doctrinal motifs distributed across the Gosvāmī corpus into a single, fully argued *acintya-bhedābheda* metaphysics, with *pramāṇa*-theory, ontology, and soteriology articulated together rather than separately. The treatise organises itself around three working questions: by what evidence (*pramāṇa*) is the doctrine known; what is its ontological commitment; and what is the *prayojana* the doctrine licenses.

On *pramāṇa*, the *Tattva-Sandarbha* argues that *śabda* — scriptural testimony — is the operative *pramāṇa* for the matter at hand, since the relations under investigation exceed *pratyakṣa* and are not derivable by *tarka* alone. Within *śabda*, the *Bhāgavata-Purāṇa* is established as the natural commentary on the *Brahma-Sūtras* and the highest scriptural authority for the school. On ontology, the *Bhagavat-Sandarbha* sets out the threefold *śakti*-doctrine: *svarūpa-śakti* (*cit*, the inner power), *jīva-śakti* (*taṭasthā*, the boundary power), *māyā-śakti* (*bahiraṅgā*, the external power). On soteriology, the *Bhakti-Sandarbha* establishes *bhakti* as the unique *abhidheya* and the *Prīti-Sandarbha* establishes *prema* — *prīti* — as the *prayojana*, the highest *puruṣārtha*.

The load-bearing concept is *acintya-śakti*. The argument is given at *Tattva-Sandarbha* §51 ff. and developed across the subsequent *Sandarbhas*. *Tarka* — dialectical reasoning — adjudicates relations of *bheda* and *abheda* by exclusion: of two terms in some respect, either they are non-identical or identical. The relation of *śakti* to *śaktimat* is one in which both *bheda* (the power is functionally distinct from its possessor) and *abheda* (the power is not a separate substance) hold non-trivially in the same respect. *Acintya-śakti* names this structural feature: the inconceivable-by-*tarka* character of the *śakti–śaktimat* relation when the *śaktimat* is Bhagavān. The *acintya* qualifier is therefore not a dodge but a positive metaphysical commitment about the limits of dialectic relative to ontology, warranted by *śabda*.

The *jīva* falls out of this structure as eternal, atomic (*aṇu*), and *taṭastha* — constitutively oriented for relation to Bhagavān, neither identical with him nor fully separable from him. *Prema*, especially in *mādhurya-rasa*, is the actualisation of this relation in its most intense form. The *Sarva-Saṃvādinī* sharpens the *Sandarbhas*' positions against rival Vedānta schools — Advaita, Viśiṣṭādvaita, Madhva-Dvaita, the Nimbārka and Bhāskara *bhedābheda* lines — and demonstrates that *acintya-bhedābheda* survives technical cross-examination on its own *pramāṇa*-theoretic and ontological terms.

engaged_works.title: Sat-Sandarbha
engaged_works.ascription_notes: Securely authored six-fold systematic Gauḍīya exposition.
engaged_works.summary: The *Ṣaṭ-Sandarbha* is the foundational systematic statement of Caitanya-Vaiṣṇava theology. Six interlocking *sandarbhas* ("essays") organise the work, and each does a delimited piece of philosophical work that the others depend on. *Tattva-Sandarbha* establishes the *pramāṇa* foundation: *śabda* is the operative *pramāṇa* for the matter at hand, and within *śabda* the *Bhāgavata-Purāṇa* is the natural commentary on the *Brahma-Sūtras*. *Bhagavat-Sandarbha* establishes Bhagavān as *svayaṃ-bhagavān* and the threefold *śakti*-doctrine — *svarūpa*, *taṭasthā*, *māyā* — with *acintya-śakti* as load-bearing concept (the §51 ff. argument that fixes the school's reading of *bheda* and *abheda* as held simultaneously by inconceivable power). *Paramātma-Sandarbha* treats Bhagavān's immanent aspect as *antaryāmin*, the *jīva*'s ontological status as eternal *taṭastha*, and the relation of Brahman to the cosmos. *Kṛṣṇa-Sandarbha* defends the school-defining *kṛṣṇas tu bhagavān svayam* — Kṛṣṇa as *svayaṃ-bhagavān*, not as *avatāra* — by extensive *Bhāgavata* citation and dialectical reply to the alternative readings. *Bhakti-Sandarbha* analyses *bhakti* as the unique *abhidheya*. *Prīti-Sandarbha* treats *prema* as *prayojana* and the highest *puruṣārtha*. The work is the canonical Gauḍīya systematic theology and the principal channel by which Caitanya's teaching enters the Sanskrit philosophical mainstream on the Sanskrit mainstream's terms.

engaged_works.title: Sarva-Samvadini
engaged_works.summary: The *Sarva-Saṃvādinī* is Jīva's auto-commentary and supplementary technical treatise on the first four *sandarbhas* of the *Ṣaṭ-Sandarbha*. The division of labour with the *Sandarbhas* themselves is structural. Where the *Sandarbhas* are organised by topic and proceed by a combined doxographical and exegetical method, the *Sarva-Saṃvādinī* sharpens the points at which Gauḍīya doctrine is exposed to technical objection — from Advaita, Viśiṣṭādvaita, Madhva-Dvaita, and the Nimbārka and Bhāskara *bhedābheda* lines — and supplies the formal *uttarapakṣa* in the technical idiom each opponent prefers. The engagements are closely focused. *Pramāṇa*-theoretic controversies: the status of *śabda* against the Naiyāyika and against the Mīmāṃsaka. Ontological controversies: the nature of Brahman's *śaktis*, the structure of the simultaneous *bheda*–*abheda* relation, the *jīva*'s *taṭastha* condition, the metaphysics of *acintya-śakti* against the charge of incoherence. Read alongside the *Sandarbhas*, the *Sarva-Saṃvādinī* forms the school's mature philosophical instrument and constitutes the explicit demonstration that *acintya-bhedābheda* is not a retreat from dialectic but a position that survives sustained Vedāntic cross-examination on its own terms.
